# SPEC-FE-AUTH-002: Child Authentication Module

## Overview

Implement authentication module for the child-facing application, focusing on device binding via invite codes and simplified session management.

**Business Context**: Children don't use traditional login credentials. Instead, they authenticate by entering a 6-character invite code generated by their parent, which binds their device to their profile.

**Target Users**:
- Primary: Children aged 6-12 with existing parent-created profiles
- Secondary: Parents helping children set up the app

---

## Technical Constraints

### Framework and Versions
- Next.js 22.x with App Router
- React 19.x
- Zustand for state management
- Device fingerprinting for session binding

### Dependencies
- SPEC-DESIGN-001 (Child App Design)
- SPEC-DESIGN-003 (Design System)
- SPEC-BE-AUTH-001 (Backend Auth API)

---

## Functional Requirements (EARS Format)

### Ubiquitous Requirements

**UR-001**: The system shall display a welcome screen with animated branding when a new child user visits the app.

```
Given a new child user opens the app
When the welcome screen loads
Then animated logo and welcoming message are displayed
And a "Let's Go!" button is visible
```

**UR-002**: The system shall provide a 6-character invite code input with auto-advance.

```
Given a child is on the device binding screen
When they view the code input
Then 6 individual input boxes are displayed
And input auto-advances to next box
And backspace moves to previous box
```

**UR-003**: The system shall display age-appropriate error messages for invalid codes.

```
Given a child enters an invalid code
When they submit
Then a friendly error message is displayed
And the message uses simple, encouraging language
```

### Event-Driven Requirements

**EDR-001**: When a child enters a valid invite code, the system shall bind the device to their profile.

```
Given a child enters a 6-character code
When the code is valid and unused
Then the device is registered to the child's profile
And a success celebration animation plays
And the child is redirected to their home dashboard
```

**EDR-002**: When a child enters an already-used code, the system shall display a friendly error.

```
Given a child enters a code
When the code was already used on another device
Then "This code was already used. Ask your parent for a new one." is displayed
And the input is cleared for retry
```

**EDR-003**: When a child enters an invalid code, the system shall display guidance.

```
Given a child enters a code
When the code doesn't exist in the system
Then "Hmm, that code doesn't look right. Try again!" is displayed
And the input shakes to indicate error
```

**EDR-004**: When a child with existing session opens the app, the system shall restore their session.

```
Given a child has previously bound their device
When they open the app
Then their session is restored automatically
And they are taken directly to their home dashboard
```

**EDR-005**: When a child taps "Need Help?", the system shall display help options.

```
Given a child is on the code input screen
When they tap "Need Help?"
Then a modal opens with options
And options include "Ask Parent" and "Try Again"
```

### State-Driven Requirements

**SDR-001**: While the code is being validated, the system shall display a loading animation.

```
Given a child has entered 6 characters
When the system is validating
Then a playful loading animation is displayed
And the submit button shows a spinner
```

**SDR-002**: While a child is authenticated, the system shall display their profile info in the header.

```
Given a child has successfully authenticated
When they view any screen
Then their avatar is visible in the header
And their name is displayed
```

**SDR-003**: While a child's session is invalid or revoked, the system shall redirect to device binding.

```
Given a child's session was revoked by parent
When they try to use the app
Then they are redirected to the device binding screen
And a friendly message explains what happened
```

### Optional Requirements

**OR-001**: The system MAY support PIN/biometric authentication for returning users.

```
Given a child has been authenticated before
When they open the app after being away
Then they may be asked for a simple 4-digit PIN
Or use device biometrics if available
```

**OR-002**: The system MAY support multiple child profiles on a single device.

```
Given multiple children share a device
When they open the app
Then they can select their profile
And switch between profiles
```

### Unwanted Behavior Requirements

**UBR-001**: The system shall NOT require children to remember passwords.

```
Given a child is authenticating
When they use the app
Then no password entry is required
And only the invite code is used for initial binding
```

**UBR-002**: The system shall NOT store sensitive data unencrypted on the device.

```
Given a child's session is established
When session data is stored locally
Then all sensitive data is encrypted
And device-specific keys are used
```

**UBR-003**: The system shall NOT allow children to see or modify invite codes.

```
Given a child is using the app
When they navigate through screens
Then no invite codes are visible
And no code management options are available
```

---

## Technical Solution

### Component Structure

```
src/
├── app/
│   ├── (child)/
│   │   ├── welcome/
│   │   │   └── page.tsx
│   │   ├── bind/
│   │   │   └── page.tsx
│   │   └── (authenticated)/
│   │       └── layout.tsx
├── components/
│   ├── child-auth/
│   │   ├── WelcomeScreen.tsx
│   │   ├── InviteCodeInput.tsx
│   │   ├── CodeInputBox.tsx
│   │   ├── HelpModal.tsx
│   │   ├── SuccessCelebration.tsx
│   │   └── ProfileSwitcher.tsx
│   └── ui/
│       └── (design system components)
├── lib/
│   ├── child-auth/
│   │   ├── device-binding.ts
│   │   ├── session.ts
│   │   └── validators.ts
│   └── hooks/
│       ├── useChildAuth.ts
│       └── useChildSession.ts
└── stores/
    └── child-auth-store.ts
```

### Invite Code Input Component

```tsx
// InviteCodeInput.tsx
interface InviteCodeInputProps {
  length?: number;
  onComplete: (code: string) => void;
  onError: (error: string) => void;
}

// Behavior:
// - Auto-focus first box on mount
// - Auto-advance on input
// - Backspace goes to previous
// - Paste support (fills all boxes)
// - Uppercase transformation
// - Error shake animation
```

### State Management

```typescript
// child-auth-store.ts
interface ChildAuthState {
  child: Child | null;
  deviceBound: boolean;
  isLoading: boolean;
  error: string | null;

  // Actions
  bindDevice: (code: string) => Promise<void>;
  checkSession: () => Promise<void>;
  clearSession: () => void;
  clearError: () => void;
}
```

### Device Binding Flow

```typescript
// device-binding.ts
export async function bindDevice(code: string): Promise<BindResult> {
  // 1. Get device fingerprint
  const fingerprint = await getDeviceFingerprint();

  // 2. Call bind API
  const result = await apiClient.post('/api/v1/child/bind', {
    code: code.toUpperCase(),
    deviceId: fingerprint,
  });

  // 3. Store session
  if (result.success) {
    await storeChildSession(result.session, result.child);
  }

  return result;
}
```

### Route Protection

```typescript
// (authenticated)/layout.tsx
export default function ChildAuthenticatedLayout({ children }) {
  const { child, deviceBound, isLoading } = useChildAuth();
  const router = useRouter();

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!isLoading && !deviceBound) {
      router.push('/welcome');
    }
  }, [deviceBound, isLoading]);

  if (isLoading) {
    return <LoadingAnimation />;
  }

  if (!deviceBound) {
    return null;
  }

  return (
    <div className="child-app-container">
      <ChildHeader child={child} />
      {children}
      <BottomNavigation />
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Animation Specifications

```typescript
// SuccessCelebration.tsx
const celebrationConfig = {
  confetti: {
    particleCount: 50,
    spread: 70,
    colors: ['#FF6B6B', '#4ECDC4', '#FFE66D', '#95E1A3'],
  },
  duration: 2000,
  sound: '/sounds/success.mp3', // Optional
};

// Success flow:
// 1. Confetti burst from center
// 2. "Yay! You're all set!" text fades in
// 3. Mascot animation (optional)
// 4. Auto-redirect to home after 2 seconds
```

---

## Risks and Mitigations

| Risk | Impact | Probability | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-------------|------------|
| Code sharing between siblings | Medium | Medium | Single-use codes, parent can regenerate |
| Device loss/replacement | High | Medium | Parent can revoke and reissue codes |
| Fingerprinting reliability | Medium | Low | Fallback to stored session token |
| Age-inappropriate complexity | Medium | Low | User testing with target age group |

---

## Acceptance Criteria

### Welcome Flow
- [ ] Given new child, when app opens, then welcome animation plays
- [ ] Given welcome complete, when "Let's Go!" tapped, then navigate to bind screen

### Code Entry
- [ ] Given code input, when typing, then auto-advance works
- [ ] Given all 6 characters, when valid, then device binds successfully
- [ ] Given invalid code, when submitted, then friendly error shows
- [ ] Given used code, when submitted, then specific error shows

### Session Persistence
- [ ] Given bound device, when app reopened, then session restored
- [ ] Given revoked session, when app opened, then redirect to bind

### Error Handling
- [ ] Given network error, when binding fails, then retry option available
- [ ] Given help needed, when "Need Help?" tapped, then modal shows

### Accessibility
- [ ] All inputs have proper labels for screen readers
- [ ] Error messages are announced
- [ ] Focus management is correct
